section_name: Failure of operator of commercial motor vehicle to slow down and check tracks
chapter_title: Rules of the Rd. for Drivers
text:
(1) A person commits the offense of failure of the operator of a commercial motor vehicle to slow down and check that tracks are clear of an approaching train or other on-track equipment if the person:

(a) Is operating a commercial motor vehicle that is not required by ORS 811.460 (Failure to follow rail crossing procedures for high-risk vehicles) to stop before reaching a rail crossing;

(b) Is approaching a rail crossing at grade; and

(c) Fails to slow down and check that the tracks are clear of an approaching train or other on-track equipment before proceeding across the railroad tracks.

(2) The offense described in this section, failure of the operator of a commercial motor vehicle to slow down and check that tracks are clear of an approaching train or other on-track equipment, is a Class B traffic violation. [2001 c.492 §7; 2017 c.176 §3]
markdown:
## Title 59
### Chapter 811. Rules of the Rd. for Drivers
##### § 811.462 — Failure of operator of commercial motor vehicle to slow down and check tracks

(1) A person commits the offense of failure of the operator of a commercial motor vehicle to slow down and check that tracks are clear of an approaching train or other on-track equipment if the person:

    (a) Is operating a commercial motor vehicle that is not required by ORS 811.460 (Failure to follow rail crossing procedures for high-risk vehicles) to stop before reaching a rail crossing;

    (b) Is approaching a rail crossing at grade; and

    (c) Fails to slow down and check that the tracks are clear of an approaching train or other on-track equipment before proceeding across the railroad tracks.

(2) The offense described in this section, failure of the operator of a commercial motor vehicle to slow down and check that tracks are clear of an approaching train or other on-track equipment, is a Class B traffic violation. [2001 c.492 §7; 2017 c.176 §3]
